rules.title: Dropdown affix must have an accessible label
rules.description: A .input-affix--dropdown button must have a non-empty accessible name via visible text in .input-affix__text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby.
rules.expected: element has accessible name via text content OR aria-label OR aria-labelledby

conditions: element has non-empty trimmed text content in .input-affix__text
element has aria-labelledby whose target has non-empty text content

rules.message: Dropdown affix has no accessible label. Add visible text in .input-affix__text or set aria-label on the button.
rules.fix: Ensure .input-affix__text contains visible text, or add aria-label="Currency selector" to the root button.

rules.title: Icon and arrow slots must be aria-hidden
rules.description: The .input-affix__icon and .input-affix__arrow SVG elements are decorative. They must carry aria-hidden='true' and focusable='false' to suppress redundant announcements.

conditions: element has focusable='false' OR element has no tabindex

rules.message: Affix icon or arrow is missing aria-hidden='true'. Screen readers will announce decorative SVG content.
rules.fix: Add aria-hidden="true" focusable="false" to the SVG element.

rules.title: Focus ring must not be suppressed
rules.description: Dropdown affix must display a visible focus indicator on :focus-visible. outline: none without a replacement focus style is forbidden.
rules.expected: element has visible outline or box-shadow focus indicator

check.forbidden: outline: none without replacement focus style

rules.message: Focus ring is suppressed on dropdown affix. Keyboard users cannot determine which element has focus.
rules.fix: Remove outline: none, or add a visible replacement: outline: var(--input-affix-focus-ring-width) solid var(--input-affix-focus-ring-color).

rules.title: Dropdown affix must declare aria-expanded when controlling a panel
rules.description: If the dropdown affix triggers a select or dropdown panel, it must communicate the expanded state via aria-expanded. Value must be 'true' or 'false'.
rules.message: Dropdown affix is missing aria-expanded. Add aria-expanded='false' by default and toggle to 'true' when the panel opens.
rules.fix: Add aria-expanded="false" to the button. Update to "true" via JavaScript when the dropdown panel is open.

rules.title: Correct root element must be used per type
rules.description: Dropdown affix (.input-affix--dropdown) must use <button> as root. Text affix (.input-affix--text) must use <div> as root. No other elements are valid.
rules.message: input-affix uses an incorrect root element. Dropdown type requires <button>. Text type requires <div>.
rules.fix: Change the root element to <button type="button"> for Dropdown, or <div> for Text type.

rules.title: No hardcoded visual values in component CSS
rules.description: All color, spacing, radius, and font values in input-affix.css must use --input-affix-* CSS custom properties. Hardcoded hex, rgba, px values for visual properties are forbidden.
rules.expected: no hardcoded color, spacing, radius, or font values on component selectors
rules.message: Hardcoded visual value found in input-affix component selector. Replace with --input-affix-* token.
rules.fix: Replace the hardcoded value with a var(--input-affix-*) token. Add the token to token.css if missing.

rules.title: No physical horizontal padding on root element
rules.description: input-affix.css must not use padding-left or padding-right on the root selector. Use padding-inline instead. Physical properties break RTL layout.
rules.expected: padding-left and padding-right are not used on root or modifier selectors
rules.message: Physical padding property (padding-left or padding-right) found on input-affix. Use padding-inline instead.

rules.title: Affix must be used within a text-input context
rules.description: input-affix is a subcomponent. It must appear as a sibling or child of an <input> or .text-input element. An isolated affix without an associated input is a usage violation.
rules.expected: element is a sibling or descendant of an <input> or .text-input element

conditions: element has sibling that is an <input>
element has ancestor with class text-input

rules.message: input-affix appears in isolation without an associated text-input. This is a usage violation.
rules.fix: Wrap the affix and its associated input inside a .text-input container.
